title: Abraham's Journey
description: A first-pass route from Ur to Haran to Canaan, designed to prove the 3D Journey data model and stop interactions.
caution: Route and stop placement are simplified for an MVP and should not be treated as a precise historical reconstruction.

stops.name: Ur
stops.description: The starting point of Abraham's family journey in southern Mesopotamia.
stops.notes: Starting point from the Genesis narrative and later interpretive tradition.

stops.name: Haran
stops.description: The northern stop where Abraham's household remained before the call to continue.
stops.notes: A major staging point for movement between Mesopotamia and the Levant.

stops.name: Shechem / Canaan
stops.description: The land where Abraham arrived and the promise was reaffirmed.
stops.notes: Representative arrival point in the land of Canaan.

segments.label: South to Haran
segments.description: Abram's family leaves Ur and settles in Haran.
segments.caution: The exact route is reconstructed from geography and ancient travel corridors.

segments.label: Into Canaan
segments.description: Abram continues from Haran into the land of Canaan.
segments.caution: This leg summarizes a longer migration movement rather than a single known road.
